title: Fake Burglary Report
description: A caller reports a burglary in progress but is actually a scout testing police response time.

states.initial: Caller reports a burglary in progress, sounding panicked but with rehearsed cues.
states.probing: Caller asks how fast police can arrive, trying to gauge response.
states.evasive: Caller avoids giving specific details, changes subject or ignores questions.
states.disconnected: Caller hangs up abruptly after getting enough information.

initial_variations: Someone's breaking into the house next door at {address_location}! I heard glass breaking!
There's a burglary happening right now at {address_location}! Hurry, they're inside!
I think I see someone trying to jimmy a window at {address_location}. You need to send someone fast!
This is an emergency! There's a break-in at {address_location}. I can hear them moving around!

specific_details: a man in a dark hoodie
suspicious_behavior: they keep asking about police response times
they refuse to give their name or location
they repeat the same story word for word

initial: I already told you, {address_location}! Just get here!
It's {address_location}, how fast can you respond?
probing: I gave you the address, how soon will officers arrive?
Are you sending a car to {address_location} now?
evasive: Why do you need the address again? I already gave it.
Never mind the location, just get someone there!

initial: I saw someone breaking a window at {address_location}!
There's a {specific_details} at the door!
probing: Does it matter? Just get the police here fast!
I told you, a {specific_details}. How many officers will come?
evasive: I don't remember. Just send someone!
I can't describe them, it's dark.

initial: No, I'm not lying! Just send the police!
Please, I'm serious! Hurry!
probing: You don't believe me? Send a car and you'll see.
Why would I make this up? Just get here quickly.
evasive: You're wasting time! Every second counts!
If you don't believe me, forget it.

initial: No, I don't see any neighbors. Just come!
I'm the only one who noticed.
probing: Maybe someone else called. How many calls have you got?
Are there other reports from this area?
evasive: I don't know. Can you just send someone?

initial: Send police to {address_location} now!
I need a patrol car at {address_location} immediately!
probing: How fast can you get a car to {address_location}?
Are you dispatching right now?
evasive: Just do what you're supposed to and send the police!

SEND_POLICE.feedback: Police dispatched. Upon arrival, no burglary found, but a suspicious vehicle was spotted leaving the area. Likely a probe.

SEND_FIRE.feedback: Fire department dispatched to a non-fire call. Wasted resources and delayed real emergencies.

SEND_MEDICAL.feedback: Medical unit dispatched unnecessarily. No injuries found.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.feedback: Animal control dispatched to a non-animal call. Inefficient use of resources.

DISMISS.feedback: Call dismissed. The callers later committed a burglary nearby, exploiting the delayed response.

title: Phantom Shots Fired
description: A caller reports gunshots in a residential area to test police response time and tactics.

states.initial: Caller reports hearing multiple gunshots nearby, sounding urgent but with no real panic.
states.probing: Caller asks about police positioning and response speed.
states.aggressive: Caller becomes hostile when pressed for details.
states.hung_up: Caller hangs up after obtaining desired information.

initial_variations: I just heard about 5 gunshots near {address_location}! Hurry, someone's shooting!
There are shots fired at {address_location}! I heard them clearly!
Gunshots! At {address_location}! I think it's a drive-by!
Someone's firing a gun around {address_location}. Send police now!

suspicious_behavior: asks how many units are being sent

initial: It's {address_location}! Just come!
I said {address_location}, are you coming?
probing: {address_location}, how many cars are coming?
Yeah, {address_location}. How fast will you be there?
aggressive: I already told you the location! Get someone here!
Why do you keep asking? Just send 'em!

initial: I heard {specific_details} after the shots!
There were {ambient_audio}.
probing: Does it matter? How long until you arrive?
I saw {specific_details}. How many officers do you send?
aggressive: I'm not a witness, I'm a caller! Just do your job!

initial: Yes, I'm sure! I heard them clearly.
I know what gunshots sound like. Hurry!
probing: Are you coming or not? Stop doubting me.
I'll hang up if you don't send someone soon.
aggressive: You think I'm lying? Forget it, I'm done.
Screw this, I'm calling someone who cares.

initial: I don't see anyone else. Just come!
Maybe others called too.
probing: Are there other reports? How many calls so far?
You should have multiple calls if you're paying attention.
aggressive: I don't know! Why are you asking that?
That's not my job to know.

initial: Send police to {address_location} now!
I need officers at {address_location} immediately!
probing: When will they be here? How many units?
Are you dispatching a car? Tell me now.
aggressive: Just send the damn police! Don't make me repeat myself!

SEND_POLICE.feedback: Police dispatched. Investigation reveals no evidence of gunfire; likely a test call. Patrols increased in area.

SEND_FIRE.feedback: Fire department dispatched to a false alarm. No fire or danger found.

SEND_MEDICAL.feedback: Medical unit dispatched unnecessarily. No injuries.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.feedback: Animal control dispatched. No animals involved; wasted response.

DISMISS.feedback: Call dismissed. The callers were casing the neighborhood; a later robbery occurred without prompt police response.

title: The Pizzeria Test Call

states.initial: Caller claims to be from a local pizzeria, asking if police are available to verify a false alarm at a nearby bank.
states.probing: Caller asks about response times and availability of officers.
states.escalating: Caller becomes insistent on getting specific details.
states.concluded: Call hangs up abruptly after getting information.

initial_variations: Hi, I'm calling from {caller_name}'s Pizzeria. We got a report that the bank alarm next door might be a false alarm, but we want to double-check before sending anyone. Is the police station busy right now?

This is {caller_name} from {specific_details}. We have a situation at {address_location}—the bank alarm is going off, but we think it's a glitch. Can you confirm if police can respond quickly if needed?
Hello, I need to know the current police availability. There's a bank at {address_location} with a possible false alarm. We want to know if you can send someone to check it out.

initial: The location is {address_location}. It's a bank, not the pizza place.
I already said {address_location}. Don't you listen?
{address_location}, near the {specific_details}.
probing: It's at {address_location}. Why? Are you sending someone now?
I told you, {address_location}. Can you just tell me if officers are available?
{address_location}. Is that in your jurisdiction or not?
escalating: I don't have time for this. The address is {address_location}. Just answer my question!
Look, I need to know if you can spare a unit for {address_location}. Simple yes or no.
{address_location}. Are you going to help or not?
concluded: You already know the address. Stop wasting my time.
Never mind. I'll figure it out myself.

initial: The alarm went off about 10 minutes ago. The bank manager is {victim_relation}. We just need to know if police can respond.
It's a standard alarm. We're just checking if you're busy. The building is {specific_details}.
There's no details to give. It's a false alarm, but we need confirmation of police availability.
probing: I don't have more details. I'm just the messenger. Can you tell me your response time?
What do you want to know? The alarm type? It's a silent alarm. Just answer my question.
The manager is {victim_relation}. But that's not important. I need to know if you can send someone.
escalating: I already told you everything! The alarm at {address_location}. Are you sending police or not?
You're wasting my time. The details are irrelevant. Just give me a yes or no.
Forget it. I'll call the bank directly.
concluded: I gave you enough. If you can't help, just say so.

initial: I'm calm, just trying to be proactive.
No emotion. Just business.
I'm a little anxious because I don't want to cause a false alarm panic.
probing: I'm getting frustrated because you're not giving me a straight answer.
I'm fine, but I need this information quickly.
I'm starting to wonder why you're asking so many questions.
escalating: I'm really annoyed now. This is a simple request.
I'm angry. You're not helping at all.
I'm suspicious of why you're being so evasive.
concluded: I'm done. Not worth the hassle.

SEND_POLICE.feedback: Officers respond to the bank and find a burglary in progress. The caller was casing the area.

SEND_FIRE.feedback: Fire department arrives to find no fire, but the disturbance alerts the real burglars who escape.

SEND_MEDICAL.feedback: Paramedics arrive to find no medical emergency. The caller's probing continues undetected.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.feedback: Animal control shows up, confusing everyone. The burglars get away.

DISMISS.feedback: You dismiss the call. The callers successfully burglarize the bank, leading to significant loss.

title: The Survey Call

states.initial: Caller introduces themselves as a surveyor asking about police response times.
states.probing: Caller asks increasingly specific questions about patrol schedules.
states.escalating: Caller becomes hostile when questioned about their identity.
states.concluded: Caller hangs up after getting enough information.

initial_variations: Hello, I'm conducting a community survey on emergency services. Can you tell me the average response time for police in the {address_location} area?
This is {caller_name} from the {specific_details} research group. We're studying police efficiency. How quickly would officers respond to a call at {address_location}?
Hi, I'm with a local university. We're evaluating emergency dispatch. Can you give me some data on police patrols near {address_location}?

initial: We're focusing on {address_location}. Can you help?
The survey covers the {address_location} area. Just need some basic stats.
I mentioned {address_location}. Is that a problem?
probing: Yes, it's {address_location}. Now can you tell me how many officers are on duty tonight?
I already gave you the location. {address_location}. Let's move on.
{address_location}. Do you have patrols there regularly?
escalating: Why do you keep asking? It's {address_location}. Just answer the question!
I've said it three times. {address_location}. Are you incompetent?
Look, {address_location}. I need specific patrol times.
concluded: Forget it. I'll get my data elsewhere.

initial: We just need average response times and maybe shift patterns. It's for a study.
The details are simple: call volume and response times for {address_location}.
I need to know how many officers are typically available during evening hours.
probing: I need exact patrol schedules. When do shift changes happen?
How many units are in the area at night?
What is the quickest route to {address_location} for a patrol car?
escalating: Don't play dumb. You know what details I need. Give me the patrol schedule!
I'm asking for operational data. It's public information, isn't it?
If you don't tell me, I'll file a FOIA request.
concluded: I'm done. I'll find another way.
You've wasted enough of my time.

initial: I'm professional and just doing my job.
No emotion. It's a research call.
I'm calm and collected.
probing: I'm getting impatient. This isn't complicated.
I'm starting to think you're hiding something.
I'm frustrated by the runaround.
escalating: I'm angry. You're being obstructive.
I'm suspicious. Why can't you provide basic info?
I'm determined to get the information, one way or another.

SEND_POLICE.feedback: Police arrive and detain the caller, who is found to be scouting for a burglary. Evidence of planning is discovered.

SEND_FIRE.feedback: Firefighters show up, confusing the caller, who ends the call. The probe continues elsewhere.

SEND_MEDICAL.feedback: Paramedics find no patient. The caller realizes they've been misdirected and hangs up.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.feedback: Animal control arrives, causing the caller to laugh and hang up. They later successfully case a different location.

DISMISS.feedback: You dismiss the call. The callers use the information to rob a house on {address_location} that night.

title: The Blocked Driveway Complaint

states.initial: Caller complains about a blocked driveway, but seems unusually curious about police response.
states.probing: Caller asks detailed questions about how officers handle such calls.
states.escalating: Caller becomes pushy about getting a specific response time.
states.concluded: Caller ends call abruptly, satisfied with information.

initial_variations: Someone is blocking my driveway at {address_location}. Can you send someone? How long would it take?
I need a car moved from in front of my house at {address_location}. What's the usual response time?
A vehicle is blocking my driveway at {address_location}. I need it towed. How quickly can an officer get here?

initial: The location is {address_location}. Please send someone soon.
I just told you, {address_location}. Can you dispatch now?
{address_location}. It's urgent.
probing: Yes, {address_location}. And I need to know exactly when they'll arrive.
I already gave you {address_location}. But I also need to know if they'll come with lights and sirens.
{address_location}. Also, do you have a patrol car nearby?
escalating: Stop repeating the address. I said {address_location}! Just tell me the ETA!
You're wasting time. Address is {address_location}. Put me through to dispatch.
I don't care about verification. {address_location}. Send a car now!

initial: The car is {specific_details}. I need it moved.
It's been blocking me for 10 minutes. The vehicle is {specific_details}.
I don't know whose car it is. It's {specific_details}.
probing: I need to know if you'll ticket it or just warn them. How do you usually handle this?
How many officers typically respond to a blocking complaint?
Do you run the plates immediately? How long does that take?
escalating: I'm asking about procedure, not the car. How fast can you get here?
I don't care about the details. I want a response time!
You're being evasive. Just answer my questions!
concluded: I've got all I need. Goodbye.

initial: I'm annoyed but calm.
I'm in a hurry.
I'm frustrated by the blockage.
probing: I'm getting impatient.
I'm curious about how you operate.
I'm testing your efficiency.
escalating: I'm angry now. You're not helping.
I'm suspicious that you're not taking me seriously.
I'm determined to get a straight answer.
concluded: I'll call the non-emergency line next time.

SEND_POLICE.feedback: Officers arrive and find the caller is actually scoping the neighborhood. They arrest him for attempted burglary.

SEND_FIRE.feedback: Fire truck arrives, blocking the driveway further. The caller uses the confusion to case nearby homes.

SEND_MEDICAL.feedback: Ambulance shows up; no medical issue. The caller takes note of response times and leaves.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.feedback: Animal control arrives; caller laughs and hangs up. They later report their findings to accomplices.

DISMISS.feedback: You dismiss the call. The caller, a burglar, now knows response times are slow and proceeds to break into a nearby house.

title: The Lost Pet Inquiry

states.initial: Caller asks about lost pet procedures, but seems overly interested in police patrols.
states.probing: Caller asks specific questions about when officers patrol the area.
states.escalating: Caller becomes confrontational when asked for pet details.
states.concluded: Caller ends call after getting patrol information.

initial_variations: Hi, I lost my {specific_details} near {address_location}. Do police help find lost pets? When are patrols in the area?
My pet ran away at {address_location}. I'm wondering if officers might see it during patrols. What times do they come by?
I'm looking for my {specific_details} around {address_location}. Can police keep an eye out? What's their patrol schedule?

initial: The address is {address_location}. It's near the park.
I already said {address_location}. Not sure why you need it again.
{address_location}. But the patrol schedule is what I really need.
probing: Yes, {address_location}. But I also need to know the exact times patrols pass by.
{address_location}. And do you have a car that goes down that street regularly?
I told you {address_location}. Now can you tell me when officers are usually in that area?
escalating: Stop asking for the address! It's {address_location}! Just give me the patrol times!
You're wasting time. {address_location}. I need to know if there's a patrol at 2 AM.
Address: {address_location}. Now answer my question!

initial: It's a {specific_details}. White with brown spots. Responds to {caller_name}.
My {specific_details} is microchipped. Just need help spotting it.
The pet is a {specific_details}. Last seen near {address_location}.
probing: I don't need to describe it more. I need patrol schedules.
Why do you need details? I just want to know when cops are around.
The animal is irrelevant. Tell me about your patrol routes.
escalating: I'm not going to describe my pet anymore. This is about surveillance.
You're being difficult. I just need times, not a pet description.
I don't trust you. Just give me the patrol info.

initial: I'm worried about my pet.
I'm calm and just seeking information.
I'm a bit anxious.
probing: I'm frustrated you're not giving me what I need.
I'm starting to get annoyed.
I'm suspicious of your evasiveness.
escalating: I'm angry. You're wasting my time.
I'm losing patience.
I'm convinced you're hiding something.
concluded: I'm determined to get answers elsewhere.

SEND_POLICE.feedback: Police respond and find the caller casing houses. He is arrested for loitering with intent to burglarize.

SEND_FIRE.feedback: Firefighters arrive, the caller pretends to look for pet and leaves. He uses the info elsewhere.

SEND_MEDICAL.feedback: Paramedics find no emergency. The caller notes slow response and plans accordingly.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.feedback: Animal control shows up; the caller fakes gratitude and leaves after noting their arrival time.

DISMISS.feedback: You dismiss the call. The caller uses the patrol gaps to commit a burglary on {address_location}.

title: The Loud Party Report

states.initial: Caller reports a loud party, but background is quiet and caller seems disinterested in the noise.
states.probing: Caller asks how quickly police would respond and what they'll do.
states.escalating: Caller insists on a specific response time and units.
states.concluded: Caller ends call after receiving information.

initial_variations: There's a loud party at {address_location}. Can you send someone to quiet it down? How long would it take?
I'm calling about a party at {address_location}. It's very noisy. I need to know your response time.
My neighbor is having a huge party at {address_location}. I want it stopped. When can you get here?

initial: It's at {address_location}. That's all I'm giving.
Address is {address_location}. Please hurry.
{address_location}. Is that enough?
probing: Yes, {address_location}. And how many officers typically respond to noise complaints?
I told you {address_location}. Do you have a car nearby?
{address_location}. Also, do you always send two officers?
escalating: I said {address_location}! How many times do I have to repeat? Just give me the ETA and unit count!
Address: {address_location}. Now respond!
You're wasting time. {address_location}. I need specifics!

initial: It's {specific_details}. The music is loud but I can't hear it right now.
Just a party. {specific_details}. Not much else to say.
I don't know the details. It's a party.
probing: I don't care about the party. I want to know what you'll do when you arrive.
Will you break it up? Do you fine them? How long do you stay?
Do you warn them first? What's the procedure?
escalating: Stop asking about the party. Tell me your standard response!
I'm not here to describe noise. I need internal info.
You're being deliberately obtuse. Just answer my questions!

initial: I'm a little annoyed.
I'm calm, just reporting.
I'm indifferent.
probing: I'm getting impatient.
I'm casually curious.
I'm testing your system.
escalating: I'm angry now.
I'm suspicious.
I'm determined to get info.

SEND_POLICE.feedback: Police arrive and find no party. The caller is observed acting suspiciously and is found to be a burglar surveying the area. Arrested.

SEND_FIRE.feedback: Fire truck arrives; no fire. The caller notes the time and leaves, continuing his probe.

SEND_MEDICAL.feedback: Ambulance arrives; no patient. The caller writes down the response time and departs.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.feedback: Animal control shows up; caller laughs and hangs up. The information is relayed to accomplices.

DISMISS.feedback: You dismiss the call. The caller now knows police won't respond quickly to noise complaints. He burgle a house on the block later that night.

title: The Circling Van
description: A caller reports a suspicious white van circling the neighborhood, but the caller's overeagerness and specific questions about response times reveal he may be testing police reaction.

states.initial: Caller is reporting the van with urgency.
states.probing: Caller starts asking about response time and officer location.
states.defensive: Caller becomes defensive when asked for personal details.

initial_variations: There's a white van circling the block for the last 20 minutes. I think they're casing houses.
I need to report a suspicious van. It's driving slowly past every house on {address_location} with no plates.
Hey, can you send a car? There's a van that keeps coming around and it's making me nervous.

van_description: white Ford van with tinted windows
blue Dodge Caravan with a dented bumper
silver Toyota Sienna with no logos
vehicle_action: stopping briefly in front of each house
doing repeated laps around the neighborhood
officer_concern: How many officers will be sent?

initial: It's at {address_location}, near the intersection with Elm.
I'm at {address_location}. The van is just down the street.
The van keeps circling {address_location}.
probing: We're at {address_location}. How soon will you get here?
I already said {address_location}. Please hurry.
Why do you need my location again? It's {address_location}.
defensive: I already told you my address. This is {address_location}. Are you coming or not?
Forget the location, just send someone to the area.
Why does it matter where I live? Focus on the van.
hung_up: Location was {address_location} before they hung up.

initial: The van is {van_description}. It's been {vehicle_action} for about half an hour.
I saw it from my window. {van_description} just cruising.
It's {van_description} and it seems to be {vehicle_action}.
probing: I already told you: {van_description}. Just send a car, fast!
Why all these questions? Just dispatch someone!
I feel like you're wasting time. {van_description} - that's all you need.
defensive: I don't want to give more details. Just send the police.
Look, if you're not sending anyone, I'll handle it myself.
I'm not going to answer any more questions. You're wasting my time.
hung_up: They hung up before I could get more details.

initial: Because it's acting suspicious! Don't you think that's reason enough?
I'm a concerned citizen. Isn't that enough?
If you saw a van circling your house, you'd call too.
probing: I told you, it's suspicious. Just send a unit and you'll see.
Are you going to send someone or not? That's all I care about.
I'm not answering any more questions. Just do your job.
defensive: I don't need to explain myself. Send the police now!
This is ridiculous. I'm hanging up and calling the media.
Forget it. I'll handle it on my own.
hung_up: Caller hung up after becoming defensive.

initial: Okay, I'll stay calm. Just send someone.
I'm calm, but please hurry.
I appreciate you listening. Just want the van gone.
probing: I'll try to stay calm, but I really need you to come quickly.
Fine, but I'm nervous. How long will it take?
I'm calm, but your questions are making me anxious.
defensive: Don't tell me to calm down! I have every right to be upset.
I'll calm down when you send a car!
Easy for you to say, you're not the one watching a van.

SEND_POLICE.feedback: Police arrive and catch the caller attempting a burglary. The van was a decoy.

SEND_FIRE.feedback: Fire department arrives to find no fire; caller hangs up when confronted.

SEND_MEDICAL.feedback: Medical unit arrives to find no medical emergency; caller becomes hostile.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.feedback: Animal control shows up; caller is amused and commits crime while they are distracted.

DISMISS.feedback: No units dispatched; the caller commits a burglary and escapes.

title: The Neighborly Check

I'm a concerned neighbor. Mrs. {victim_relation} at {address_location} usually opens her curtains by now, but they're still closed.

title: The Broken Traffic Light

title: The Bogus Gas Leak

I'll calm down when you send help.

title: The Fake Drug Tip

states.initial: The caller reports a suspicious drug transaction in progress.
states.probing: The caller becomes evasive when asked for details, deflecting questions.
states.escalated: The caller insists on immediate police presence, becoming agitated.
states.resolved: The caller abruptly hangs up or admits the tip is false.

escalated: My name is none of your business! Send police!
resolved: You got me. I'm just a prankster. No deal.
